title: Two typhoons head towards Japan bringing torrential rains
summary: Typhoons 7 and 8 advance towards the Japanese archipelago, with warnings of floods and landslides in wide areas of the country.

title: Japan will invest 370 trillion yen in 17 strategic areas to boost growth
summary: The government plan includes sectors such as artificial intelligence and semiconductors, with the aim of breaking the trend of insufficient investment.

title: Japan fine-tunes its strategy for the crucial match against Sweden in the World Cup
summary: International betting gives Japan a 53% chance of victory, while the team adjusts its tactical formation to secure passage to the round of 16.

title: The Emperor and Empress of Japan conclude their state visit to Belgium and the Netherlands
summary: During the tour, the monarchs highlighted the historical and cultural ties between Japan and the European kingdoms.

title: China detains two Japanese for attempting to take rare earths out of the country
summary: Fuji Electric employees were arrested for alleged smuggling, causing unease in the Japanese business sector.

title: Parliament advances reform that allows adopting male heirs for imperial succession
summary: The proposal seeks to guarantee dynastic stability through exceptional adoptions of members of collateral imperial families.

title: The Cool Japan fund accumulates losses of 54 billion yen and the government studies its closure
summary: The public-private fund for Japanese cultural promotion has recorded deficits for years, leading the ministry to consider its dissolution.

title: Iran plans to charge tolls in the Strait of Hormuz while emergency maritime corridors are established
summary: Tensions on the key oil route continue, with more than 10,000 people awaiting evacuation and maritime traffic still reduced.

title: Fire at Tokyo school caused by a teacher drying clothes on a heater
summary: The fire destroyed a classroom and authorities are investigating the incident as possible negligence, though there were no injuries.

title: Special prosecutor to be tried for conducting illegal interrogation with threats and shouting
summary: The Tokyo court considered that the prosecutor violated the penal code by coercing a suspect, in a case that has generated debate about ethics in the prosecution.

title: LDP and Ishin submit joint proposal to review national security documents
summary: The two political groups delivered recommendations to the prime minister for updating the defense strategy, although disagreements persist.

title: Government plans to temporarily reduce consumption tax to zero for families with children
summary: The measure would include bonuses for each child under 15 years old for two years, but the source of funding is not yet defined.

title: France reaches 44.3 degrees in the most intense heatwave in its history
summary: The high temperatures have caused dozens of deaths by drowning in France and Italy, which has prohibited outdoor work.

title: Oil falls below $70 per barrel for the first time in three months
summary: The decline is due to the easing of tensions in the Middle East and expectations of lower global demand.

title: Global markets close lower dragged down by crude oil decline and uncertainty
summary: The Japanese Nikkei lost more than 600 points, while Wall Street and European markets also recorded losses.

title: Japan strengthens its defense alliances with Canada, United Kingdom and Italy
summary: The Defense Minister seeks to strengthen military cooperation in a context of global uncertainty and regional tensions.

title: Honda, Nissan and Mitsubishi will standardize key components for next-generation vehicles
summary: The three manufacturers agreed to share drivetrain parts to reduce costs and increase competitiveness.

title: Nissan shareholders deny re-election of an external director with only 48% of votes in favor
summary: Director Nagai did not reach the necessary majority, reflecting discontent over management and the drop in share value.

title: KADOKAWA board ratifies president despite attempts to remove him by the main shareholder
summary: Oasis fund did not succeed in removing Takeshi Natsuno, who was reelected amid tensions over corporate strategy.

title: Japanese localities offer subsidies to open bookstores and combat lack of access to books
summary: Municipal initiatives seek to recover bookstores as basic cultural infrastructure in areas without book supply.
